news.title: Jeff Jackson's Baby Fully Grown
news.description: Jeff Jackson was the first head coach of the U.S. National Team Development Program, leaving Lake Superior State to start the program after having won two national championships there as a head coach, and another as an assistant. He was much more than that, though. He had to start the entire program, including figuring out exactly what its mission would be, how it would operate, how it would attract...

news.title: Team of the Week: Bentley
news.description: Last season, Bentley had its first 20-win season, first Atlantic Hockey Tournament championship, and first NCAA Tournament appearance in program history. Those were heady times.But this season, as it approaches 20 wins again (18-9-5), has been about not stopping that momentum.To that end, Bentley took another step, and maybe the hardest one yet, when it defeated RIT last weekend to wrap up the Atlantic...

news.title: Bracket Breakdown
news.description: I've been writing these NCAA Tournament analysis articles for decades now, and over the years, they've become increasingly routine.That's largely because the makeup of what was the Pairwise became more and more straightforward over the years.And now, with the move to NPI, the algorithm is entirely straightforward.For better or worse (and a few coaches, but not most, have said it's worse), the NPI ...

news.title: Boston University Baffled By Inability to Pull Things Together
news.description: Consistency, and lack thereof, has defined the Boston University Terriers this season.“Our team’s having a tough time sustaining the same type of play for the whole game,” coach Jay Pandolfo said after BU’s 6-2 loss to Boston College in the Beanpot Final on Feb. 9.With three games left in the regular season, their record stands at a disappointing 14-15-2. Currently 28th on the NPI, the only...

news.title: Upton Named Next Brown Coach
news.description: Brown has named Tom Upton its new head coach, replacing Brendan Whittet, who stepped down last week.Upton is currently an assistant coach at Massachusetts. He will remain there through the end of the season, while Jason Smith continues as Brown's interim head coach."Tom's track record as a skillful and proven leader, a respected builder of programs and culture, and a strong connector made him the ...

news.title: Ohio State Battling to Make Something of Tough Season
news.description: Despite Ohio State losing critical pieces over the offseason, its roster still looked solid on paper and good enough to keep up with any team in the nation. The Buckeyes' top-6 was solid, outside of losing their leading scorer, their back end was still steady and their goaltending had potential to be really good.“When you have some change over, you're not sure where it's going to come from. And ...

news.title: Staying Power
news.description: North Dakota fans themselves might not get too much sympathy from others for what may feel like longstanding suffering, but the reality for the Fighting Hawks is this: since winning the national championship a decade ago, UND hasn't returned to the Frozen Four, and in fact missed the NCAA Tournament entirely in two of the past three seasons.But up until 2016, North Dakota was no stranger to the big...

news.title: Maryville Set to Formally Announce Start of New Program
news.description: Maryville University has received approval to launch its new Division I hockey program. An official announcement is expected in the coming week.The school announced last year at the Frozen Four in St. Louis that it would upgrade to NCAA D-I if it could meet a fundraising goal of $5.5 million. The funding was needed to upgrade its facilities.Maryville recently closed in on $4 million towards its goal...

news.title: McKenna's 8-Point Night Most in NCAA in 39 Years
news.description: Gavin McKenna has had his ups and downs this season, but Friday's on-ice performance ranks as spectacular.McKenna had 1 goal and 7 assists (5 primaries) in Penn State's 11-4 win over Ohio State. The 8 points in one game are the most since CHN started recording game-by-game statistics. The last player to have seven in a game was Maine's Brian Flynn, in 2010.It's believed the last player in an NCAA ...

news.title: Hagens, Martone In Sync From Across the Country
news.description: In a bit of college hockey synchronicity, blue chippers James Hagens of Boston College and Porter Martone of Michigan State scored hat tricks in games Friday, and in each case, the player reached his 20th goal of the season in doing so.Martone and Hagens were also the No. 6 and 7 overall picks in the 2025 NHL Draft.Martone, a freshman who made the jump from Major Junior to the NCAA after getting drafted...

news.title: Bentley Captures Program's First Regular-Season Championship
news.description: Bentley defeated RIT on Friday, clinching the program's first regular-season championship.Bentley is up eight points on RIT with just two games remaining for each team.Last season, Bentley won its first-ever postseason championship, when it won the Atlantic Hockey Tournament and went on to the NCAA Tournament.After finishing 23-15-2 last season, the Falcons are currently 18-9-5 and No. 25 in the NPI...

news.title: Here's Johnny!
news.description: In 1789, Benjamin Franklin penned his famous quote about inevitability, noting that nothing — including, at the time, the future of the United States — was certain, "except death and taxes."Well, he didn't know about goaltending at the University of Denver — a certainty if there ever was one.Only three months ago, freshman Quentin Miller, the latest in Denver's long succession of goaltenders...

news.title: This Week in College Hockey: Feb. 19, 2026
news.description: It’s always worth pausing to appreciate the long arcs of player development, especially when the payoff arrives.Brandon Bussi left Western Michigan four years ago and signed with the Boston Bruins. news.title: Gundarah's Journey
news.description: Around the age of 17, the parents of Sacred Heart’s Ajeet Gundarah moved to Canada from India, the country where they were born. The Gundarahs settled into Richmond, B.C., a city 10 miles, or 16.2 kilometers, south of Vancouver.With a heavy Southeast Asian demographic, the area was a comfortable landing spot for the Gundarah family and for Ajeet growing up.And thus blossomed a love of hockey.“...

news.title: Valuable Valente
news.description: Liam Valente put up 20 points over his two seasons at Providence, as the Swedish forward skated in 59 games for the Friars.Valente committed to Providence relatively late in the game and had never previously considered college hockey. However, one of his best friends and childhood teammates – Philip Svedebäck – was a Providence commit, and the Friars expressed interest in the left-shot forward...

news.title: Surging Minnesota State Eyes Top-4 Finish
news.description: With four regular-season games left before the CCHA’s Mason Cup Playoffs, the Minnesota State Mavericks sit on the outside looking in at home-ice advantage.For Minnesota State, a program that has won the conference three times since the CCHA reformed in 2021-22, finishing outside of the top four would be something unfamiliar. A 16-9-5 record has the Mavericks sitting at 41 points, where they slimly...

news.title: Brown Coaching Search Heating Up
news.description: Brown has narrowed its coaching choices to four, according to Providence hockey reporter Mark Divver.According to his report, Brown is zeroing in on Dartmouth assistant Jason Tapp; Brown alum Scott Ford, who is an assistant coach in the NHL with Columbus; Tommy Upton, an assistant at Massachusetts; and Arizona State assistant Dana Borges.Current Brown coach Brendan Whittet announced he was stepping...

news.title: Wisconsin Trying to Flip the Script Back, Just in Time For Postseason
news.description: Mike Hastings was hired as the head coach at Wisconsin on March 30, 2023, following the release of Tony Granato.
